symptom: name extraction regex mishandled em-dash in 'Build a 2D platformer called X — 3 levels' — the capture group stopped mid-name or swallowed parameter detail
root_cause: original regex '(called|named|titled)\s+(\S+(?:\s+\S+){0,4})' greedy-consumed past the dash; subsequent tightening landed in stages (e056b66 → 4f8094a → d99baf5) without a regression anchor
fix_approach: extract derive_project_name() into tsunami/pre_scaffold_naming.py; pin the regex + fallback behavior with a corpus of 15+ prompts so a future regex refactor can't silently drift it
principle: v7 — formalization exposes drift; once the regex has a test corpus, edits are forced to stay aware of the cases

prompt: Build a 2D platformer called Lava Leap

desc: em-dash separates name from parameter detail
prompt: Build a 2D platformer called Lava Leap — 3 levels
Build a platformer called Ice Cavern – 5 rooms
Build a game called Silent Blade - stealth mission
Build a game titled Crystal Saga: return of the king
Build a platformer called X — 3 levels
Build a dashboard named Analytics Hub
Build a game titled Neon Drift

desc: 4-word name (regex allows up to 4 Capital tokens)
prompt: Build a game called The Last Starfighter Alpha

desc: no 'called' keyword — fallback takes first 4 words
prompt: Build a racing game with 5 tracks

desc: lowercase in 'called' target → fallback path (regex requires leading capital; 'called' is dropped as noise word)
prompt: Build a game called darkness rising

desc: name with digits embedded (regex allows A-Za-z0-9)
prompt: Build a game titled Quake2 Arena

desc: regex stops at non-alphanumeric symbol in capture

prompt: Build a game called ABCDEFGHIJ Klmnopqrst Uvwxyza Bcdefghij Klmnopqr
